questions.q: To which god did the bow Pinaka originally belong?
questions.explanation: The Pinaka was the mighty bow of the god Shiva, later kept in Janaka's court.

questions.q: In whose court was the Pinaka bow kept?
questions.explanation: The bow was preserved in the court of King Janaka of Mithila.

questions.q: What was the prize for stringing the Pinaka bow?
options: The hand of Sita in marriage
questions.explanation: Janaka vowed that whoever could string the bow would win the hand of his daughter Sita.

questions.q: Which prince ultimately strung the Pinaka bow?
questions.explanation: Rama lifted and strung the bow, fulfilling the condition to marry Sita.

questions.q: What was remarkable about the Pinaka that made it a fitting test?
options: It could not be lifted by any king
questions.explanation: The colossal bow was so heavy that no king could even lift it.

questions.q: Whom did Rama marry after stringing the Pinaka?
questions.explanation: By stringing the bow, Rama won the hand of Sita, Janaka's daughter.

questions.q: What happened to the Pinaka bow when Rama drew it to string it?
questions.explanation: As Rama bent the bow to string it, the Pinaka snapped in two with a thunderous sound.

questions.q: Which sage led Rama and Lakshmana to Janaka's court where the bow was kept?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra brought the young princes to Mithila to witness Janaka's great bow.

questions.q: The Pinaka is most closely associated with which type of object in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Pinaka is famous as a bow, the celebrated weapon of Shiva.

questions.q: In which kingdom did the contest to string the Pinaka take place?
questions.explanation: The bow contest was held in Mithila, the kingdom ruled by King Janaka.

questions.q: How was the enormous Pinaka bow transported into the assembly for the contest?
options: Drawn on a large eight-wheeled cart
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's account the bow was brought in on a great eight-wheeled iron chest drawn by many men.

questions.q: How did King Janaka come to possess the Pinaka bow?
options: It was a gift from the gods to his ancestors
questions.explanation: The bow was given by the gods to Janaka's ancestor Devarata and passed down as a family heirloom.

questions.q: How was Sita described in relation to her birth, the princess won through the Pinaka?
options: Found in a furrow of the earth
questions.explanation: Sita was found by Janaka in a furrow while ploughing a field, hence called daughter of the earth.

questions.q: What did King Janaka do after Rama broke the Pinaka, before the wedding?
questions.explanation: Janaka dispatched envoys to Ayodhya to invite Dasharatha for the marriage of Rama and Sita.

questions.q: Which mighty figure had previously failed to bend or lift the Pinaka, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that even the powerful Ravana could not lift or string the great bow of Shiva.

questions.q: What sound accompanied the breaking of the Pinaka bow?
questions.explanation: The bow snapped with a crash compared to a thunderbolt, heard far across the land.

questions.q: What did Rama do to the bow before it broke?
options: He shot an arrow with it
He lifted, bent, and began to string it
questions.explanation: Rama effortlessly lifted the bow, bent it to fasten the string, and in doing so it broke.

questions.q: Why did Janaka set the bow-stringing as a condition for Sita's marriage?
options: To find a husband of matchless strength worthy of her
questions.explanation: Janaka wished his daughter to be wed only to a man of extraordinary valor, proven by the bow.

questions.q: How is the worship of the Pinaka described in Janaka's court before the contest?
options: It was used daily for hunting
It was honored with offerings of perfume and incense
questions.explanation: Janaka venerated the divine bow with regular offerings of incense, perfume, and reverence.

questions.q: Which of Rama's three brothers also married daughters of Janaka's family at the same ceremony?
questions.explanation: All three brothers wed: Lakshmana married Urmila, while Bharata and Shatrughna married Janaka's nieces.

questions.q: What was the general reaction of the assembled kings who could not lift the Pinaka?
questions.explanation: The many kings who failed even to stir the bow departed in shame and disappointment.

questions.q: The breaking of the Pinaka set the stage for an encounter with which fierce sage-warrior on the road home?
questions.explanation: News of the broken bow of Shiva drew the wrathful Parashurama to confront Rama after the wedding.

questions.q: Through which royal lineage had the Pinaka bow descended to King Janaka?
options: The line of Nimi through Devarata
questions.explanation: The bow descended through the kings of Mithila, having been entrusted to Janaka's forefather Devarata.

questions.q: According to tradition, for what purpose had Shiva once readied the Pinaka before it came to Mithila?
options: To destroy the gods at Daksha's sacrifice
questions.explanation: Shiva had bent the Pinaka in anger to destroy the gods at Daksha's sacrifice before being appeased.

questions.q: After Rama broke the Pinaka, which other divine bow did Parashurama challenge him to string?
questions.explanation: Parashurama brought the bow of Vishnu and challenged Rama, who strung it and thereby humbled the sage.

questions.q: How many men are said to have been required to draw the cart bearing the Pinaka in Valmiki's Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes five thousand tall, strong men straining to draw the eight-wheeled chest holding the bow.

questions.q: What name is sometimes given to Shiva himself in reference to wielding the Pinaka?
questions.explanation: Shiva is called Pinaki or Pinakapani, meaning the bearer of the Pinaka bow.

questions.q: In Valmiki's narrative, what did Janaka say about even gods and gandharvas regarding the Pinaka?
options: They had crafted it for him
They too were unable to string it
They worshipped it as a deity
questions.explanation: Janaka declared that even gods, demons, and gandharvas had been unable to string the mighty bow.

questions.q: What did Janaka offer to give as a dowry, weeping with joy after the bow was broken?
options: Sita along with great wealth and his devotion to Rama
questions.explanation: Overcome with joy, Janaka gave Sita to Rama together with abundant riches and heartfelt blessings.

questions.q: Which event involving the Pinaka is traditionally invoked to show Rama as an incarnation surpassing former heroes?
options: His effortless breaking of Shiva's bow that none could lift
His forging of a new bow
His refusal to touch the bow
His gifting of the bow to Lakshmana
questions.explanation: Rama's effortless breaking of Shiva's unliftable bow marks him as supreme among all warriors and a divine incarnation.
